author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
Manichæus Places Five Natures in the Region of Darkness.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Manichæus Places Five Natures in the Region of Darkness.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: "There dwelt," he says, "in that region fiery bodies, destructive races."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: By speaking of dwelling, he must mean that those bodies were animated and in life.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But, not to appear to cavil at a word, let us see how he divides into five classes all these inhabitants of this region.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: "Here," he says, "was boundless darkness, flowing from the same source in immeasurable abundance, with the productions properly belonging to it.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Beyond this were muddy turbid waters, with their inhabitants; and inside of them winds terrible and violent, with their prince and their progenitors.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Then, again, a fiery region of destruction, with its chiefs and peoples.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And, similarly, inside of this a race full of smoke and gloom, where abode the dreadful prince and chief of all, having around him innumerable princes, himself the mind and source of them all.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Such are the five natures of the pestiferous region."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: We find here five natures mentioned as part of one nature, which he calls the pestiferous region.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: The natures are darkness, waters, winds, fire, smoke; which he so arranges as to make darkness first, beginning at the outside.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Inside of darkness he puts the waters; inside of the waters, the winds; inside of the winds, the fire; inside of the fire, the smoke.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And each of these natures had its peculiar kind of inhabitants, which were likewise five in number.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For to the question, Whether there was only one kind in all, or different kinds corresponding to the different natures; the reply is, that they were different: as in other books we find it stated that the darkness had serpents; the waters swimming creatures, such as fish; the winds flying creatures, such as birds; the fire quadrupeds, such as horses, lions, and the like; the smoke bipeds, such as men.
